meta.description: Structured GRI disclosure requirements for GRI 302, 303, 305, 306, 403. Each disclosure lists its status, the dataset columns required, and any known data gaps.

statuses.implemented: Disclosed and sourced from dataset columns
statuses.narrative_gap: Required qualitative management narrative — cannot be auto-generated from data
statuses.data_gap: Requires dataset column(s) that do not currently exist
statuses.partial: Some years or sub-items present; others absent

302_management.title: Management approach — Energy
302_management.notes: Requires energy policy, reduction targets, and renewable energy commitment narrative. Rendered as narrative placeholder table in reports.

302_1.title: Energy consumption within the organization
302_1.notes: Conversion factors: 780 MKCal for power; 4.187 MKCal to GJ. Site-specific GCV for natural gas.

302_3.title: Energy intensity
302_3.notes: Intensity ratio: GJ per production tonne.

302_4.title: Reduction of energy consumption
302_4.notes: Computed as baseline-year (first available year) minus current year. Initiative-specific attribution requires manual input.

302_5.title: Reductions in energy requirements of products and services
302_5.notes: Requires qualitative narrative on product/service redesign for reduced energy requirements. Cannot be derived from dataset.

303_1.title: Interactions with water as a shared resource
303_1.notes: Requires water policy, stress area identification methodology, and water stewardship commitments. Rendered as narrative placeholder.

303_2.title: Management of water discharge-related impacts
303_2.notes: Requires effluent treatment approach, permit conditions, discharge standards applied, and contractor destination confirmation.

303_3.title: Water withdrawal

303_3_a.title: Total water withdrawal from all areas by source

303_3_b.title: Total water withdrawal from water stress areas by source

303_3_c.title: Total water withdrawal by water quality

303_3.notes: Per GRI 303, harvested rainwater is classified as surface water and must be included in surface water totals.

303_4_a.title: Total water discharge to all areas by destination

303_4_b.title: Total water discharge by water quality

303_4_c.title: Total water discharge to areas with water stress
303_4_c.notes: Requires linking discharge destinations to WRI Aqueduct water stress classification.

303_5_b.title: Water consumption in water stress areas
303_5_b.notes: Requires mapping each site to WRI Aqueduct and tagging consumption by stress classification.

303_5_c.title: Change in water storage
303_5_c.notes: GRI 303-5-c: Report significant changes in water stored that affect water consumption figures.

305_management.title: Management approach — Emissions
305_management.notes: Requires emissions reduction targets, GHG accounting standard, base year rationale, GWP source, Scope 2 market-based approach, and reduction initiatives.

305_1.title: Direct (Scope 1) GHG emissions
305_1.notes: GHG accounting standard and GWP source require manual confirmation.

305_2.title: Energy indirect (Scope 2) GHG emissions
305_2.notes: Location-based method implemented. Market-based method requires REC/GO data.

305_3.title: Other indirect (Scope 3) GHG emissions
305_3.notes: Category-level breakdown (GHG Protocol Categories 1-15) not tracked individually.

305_5.title: Reduction of GHG emissions
305_5.notes: Requires initiative-level GHG reduction attribution data not currently tracked in dataset.

305_6.title: Emissions of ozone-depleting substances (ODS)
305_6.notes: ODS inventory not currently tracked in dataset.

305_7.title: NOx, SOx, and other significant air emissions
305_7.notes: Data available from 2022 only. POPs, HAPs, and ODS sub-categories not tracked.

306_1.title: Waste generation and significant waste-related impacts
306_1.notes: Requires identification of waste-generating processes, significant waste types, and material topic boundary. Cannot be auto-generated.

306_2.title: Management of significant waste-related impacts
306_2.notes: Requires description of waste prevention programmes, waste hierarchy application, and contractor tracking processes.

306_3.title: Waste generated
306_3.notes: Total = Diverted + Disposed. Composition data by material type not tracked.

306_4.title: Waste diverted from disposal
306_4.notes: Onsite/offsite split and contractor destination confirmation not tracked in dataset.

306_5.title: Waste directed to disposal
306_5.notes: Onsite/offsite split, open burning flag, and contractor destination confirmation not tracked.

403_2.title: Hazard identification, risk assessment, incident investigation

403_4.title: Worker participation, consultation, and communication on OHS

403_7.title: Prevention and mitigation of OHS impacts via business relationships

403_8.title: Workers covered by an OHS management system

403_9.title: Work-related injuries
403_9.notes: Injury type breakdown (403-9-c hazard classification) requires manual OHS system input. TRIR and LTIFR computed on 200,000-hour basis.

403_10.title: Work-related ill health
403_10.notes: Ill health hazard-type breakdown (403-10-c) requires occupational disease case analysis — not auto-derivable.
